problem_text_cn:
四、(20分)
S系xy平面上，有一个沿x方向以匀速度v运动的环状物abcd，环上a，c两点连线与x轴重合。t时刻环在Oxy平面上的投影线方程为
$$(x-vt)^2+y^2=R^2,$$
t=0时刻的投影线如图所示。运用你学过的知识分析判定，在S系中：
（1）随环状物一起运动的环心点发出的所有光线，经环内壁反射后是否必定过环心点？
（2）在环状物a，c两点连线上能否找到两个随环状物一起运动的F₁点（右侧点）和F₂点（左侧点），使得F₁点发出的所有光线，经环内壁反射后必定都过F₂点？若能，试导出F₁，F₂在Oxy平面上t时刻投影点的x，y坐标。
problem_text_en:
Four. (20 points)
In the xy-plane of the S frame, there is a ring‑shaped object abcd moving uniformly in the x‑direction with speed v. The line connecting points a and c on the ring coincides with the x‑axis. At time t, the projection curve of the ring on the Oxy plane is
$$(x-vt)^2+y^2=R^2,$$
and its projection at t=0 is shown in the figure. Using the knowledge you have learned, analyze and determine in the S frame:
(1) Do all light rays emitted from the centre of the ring (moving with the ring) and reflected by the inner wall necessarily pass through the centre?
(2) On the line connecting points a and c of the ring, can we find two points F₁ (on the right) and F₂ (on the left) that move with the ring, such that all light rays emitted from F₁, after reflection from the inner wall, must pass through F₂? If possible, derive the x, y coordinates of the projection points of F₁ and F₂ on the Oxy plane at time t.
problem_solution_cn:
四、(20分)

取相对环静止的惯性系S′，由
$$ \left.\begin{matrix}x'=\frac{x-vt}{\sqrt{1-\beta^{2}}},\\[2pt]y'=y,\end{matrix}\right\}\Rightarrow\left\{\begin{matrix}x-vt=\sqrt{1-\beta^{2}}\,x',\quad\beta=\frac{v}{c},\\[2pt]y=y',\end{matrix}\right. $$
得环在S′系中的静态方程为
$$ \frac{x'^{2}}{A^{2}}+\frac{y'^{2}}{B^{2}}=1,\quad\left\{\begin{matrix}A=R/\sqrt{1-\beta^{2}}>R,\\B=R,\end{matrix}\right. $$
这是一个静止椭圆环。椭圆两个焦点$F_{1}$，$F_{2}$的坐标分别为
$$ F_{1}\{C,0\},\;F_{2}\{-C,0\},\quad C=\sqrt{A^{2}-B^{2}}=\frac{\beta R}{\sqrt{1-\beta^{2}}}.\quad(8\text{分}) $$

静态椭圆几何光学性质：
$$ \left\{\begin{matrix}1.\text{椭圆中心发出的光，经椭圆内侧反射后不能会聚到椭圆中心；}\\2.\text{椭圆一个焦点发出的光，经椭圆内侧反射后会聚到另一个焦点上。}\end{matrix}\right. $$

(1) 因对称，S′系中静态椭圆环的中心对应S系中匀速运动环状物的中心点。据静态椭圆几何光学性质1可知，S系中随环状物一起运动的环心发出的所有光线，经环内壁反射后并非都过环心点。 (4分)

(2) S′系中静态椭圆环的两个焦点$F_{1}$，$F_{2}$的坐标为
$$ F_{1}\{x_{1}'=C,\;y_{1}'=0\},\quad F_{2}\{x_{2}'=-C,\;y_{2}'=0\}, $$
经洛伦兹变换
$$ \left.\begin{matrix}x'=\frac{x-vt}{\sqrt{1-\beta^{2}}},\\[2pt]y'=y,\end{matrix}\right\}\Rightarrow\left\{\begin{matrix}x=\sqrt{1-\beta^{2}}\,x'+vt,\\[2pt]y=y',\end{matrix}\right. $$
可得S系中t时刻$F_{1}$，$F_{2}$在S系中的投影点坐标为
$$ F_{1}\{\sqrt{1-\beta^{2}}\,C+vt,\;0\},\quad F_{2}\{-\sqrt{1-\beta^{2}}\,C+vt,\;0\}. $$
将$C=\dfrac{\beta R}{\sqrt{1-\beta^{2}}}$代入，得
$$ F_{1}\left\{\frac{v}{c}R+vt,\;0\right\},\quad F_{2}\left\{-\frac{v}{c}R+vt,\;0\right\}.\qquad(8\text{分}) $$
这两个投影点都在题图中a，c连线上，对称地分居于环心投影点$\{vt,0\}$两侧。结合静态椭圆几何光学性质2可知，本小问的答案为“能”。
problem_solution_en:
Four. (20 points)

Take the inertial frame S′ that is stationary relative to the ring. From
$$ \left.\begin{matrix}x'=\frac{x-vt}{\sqrt{1-\beta^{2}}},\\[2pt]y'=y,\end{matrix}\right\}\Rightarrow\left\{\begin{matrix}x-vt=\sqrt{1-\beta^{2}}\,x',\quad\beta=\frac{v}{c},\\[2pt]y=y',\end{matrix}\right. $$
the static equation of the ring in the S′ frame becomes
$$ \frac{x'^{2}}{A^{2}}+\frac{y'^{2}}{B^{2}}=1,\quad\left\{\begin{matrix}A=R/\sqrt{1-\beta^{2}}>R,\\B=R,\end{matrix}\right. $$
which is a stationary elliptical ring. The coordinates of the two foci $F_{1}$, $F_{2}$ of the ellipse are
$$ F_{1}\{C,0\},\;F_{2}\{-C,0\},\quad C=\sqrt{A^{2}-B^{2}}=\frac{\beta R}{\sqrt{1-\beta^{2}}}.\quad(8\text{ points}) $$

Geometric‑optical properties of a stationary ellipse:
$$ \left\{\begin{matrix}1.\text{Light emitted from the centre of an ellipse, after reflection from the inner side, does not converge back to the centre;}\\2.\text{Light emitted from one focus of an ellipse, after reflection from the inner side, converges to the other focus.}\end{matrix}\right. $$

(1) By symmetry, the centre of the static elliptical ring in S′ corresponds to the centre of the uniformly moving ring in S. According to property 1 of the stationary ellipse, in the S frame not all light rays emitted from the ring centre (moving with the ring) and reflected by the inner wall will pass through the centre. (4 points)

(2) The coordinates of the two foci $F_{1}$, $F_{2}$ of the static elliptical ring in S′ are
$$ F_{1}\{x_{1}'=C,\;y_{1}'=0\},\quad F_{2}\{x_{2}'=-C,\;y_{2}'=0\}. $$
Applying the Lorentz transformation
$$ \left.\begin{matrix}x'=\frac{x-vt}{\sqrt{1-\beta^{2}}},\\[2pt]y'=y,\end{matrix}\right\}\Rightarrow\left\{\begin{matrix}x=\sqrt{1-\beta^{2}}\,x'+vt,\\[2pt]y=y',\end{matrix}\right. $$
we obtain the coordinates of the projection points of $F_{1}$, $F_{2}$ in the S frame at time t:
$$ F_{1}\{\sqrt{1-\beta^{2}}\,C+vt,\;0\},\quad F_{2}\{-\sqrt{1-\beta^{2}}\,C+vt,\;0\}. $$
Substituting $C=\dfrac{\beta R}{\sqrt{1-\beta^{2}}}$ yields
$$ F_{1}\left\{\frac{v}{c}R+vt,\;0\right\},\quad F_{2}\left\{-\frac{v}{c}R+vt,\;0\right\}.\qquad(8\text{ points}) $$
These two projection points lie on the line connecting a and c in the problem figure, symmetrically located on either side of the centre projection point $\{vt,0\}$. Together with geometric‑optical property 2 of a stationary ellipse, the answer to this sub‑question is “yes”.
img_cn:
该图展示了一个S系xy平面上的几何光学问题。图中包含一个圆环的静态投影示意图，以及相关的物理描述文字。

在图的左侧，有一个坐标系Oxy，其中x轴水平向右，y轴竖直向上。圆环的静态截面（t=0时刻）被描绘成一个以原点O为中心的标准圆。圆周上标记了四个特征点：a点位于x轴负半轴上（左端点），c点位于x轴正半轴上（右端点），b点位于y轴负半轴上（下端点），d点位于y轴正半轴上（上端点）。a和c两点的连线即为圆的直径，且与x轴完全重合。

从图示可以看出，圆环的中心位于坐标原点O(0,0)。圆的边界用实线表示，清晰地勾勒出其完整的圆形轮廓。y轴上对称地标出了b和d两个端点，虽然未明确标出半径数值，但暗示该圆环具有统一的半径R。

图的右侧是问题的文字描述部分，包括环状物的运动学方程：(x-vt)²+y²=R²，表明这是一个以速度v沿x轴正方向做匀速直线运动的圆环在t时刻的投影方程。问题要求分析两个与光的反射特性相关的物理命题，并可能涉及求解特定点的坐标位置。

整体图像简洁明了，通过几何图形与代数方程的结合，直观地呈现了运动圆环的光学问题场景。
这是一道物理题的解题过程图，主要涉及相对论和椭圆的几何光学性质。首先，取相对环静止的惯性系S'，通过坐标变换得出环在S'系中的静态方程为一个静止椭圆环，给出了椭圆的参数及两个焦点的坐标。接着阐述静态椭圆的两条几何光学性质。然后基于对称性，对S系中随环状物运动的环心发出光线的反射情况进行分析。最后通过洛伦兹变换得出S系中t时刻S'系焦点在S系中的投影点坐标，结合几何光学性质得出结论。
img_en:
The diagram illustrates a geometric‑optics problem in the xy‑plane of the S frame. It shows a static projection of a ring‑shaped object together with descriptive physics text.

On the left side, a coordinate system Oxy is drawn with the x‑axis pointing rightward and the y‑axis upward. The static cross‑section of the ring (at t=0) is depicted as a standard circle centered at the origin O. Four characteristic points are marked on the circumference: point a on the negative x‑axis (left endpoint), point c on the positive x‑axis (right endpoint), point b on the negative y‑axis (bottom endpoint), and point d on the positive y‑axis (top endpoint). The line connecting a and c is the diameter of the circle and coincides exactly with the x‑axis.

The centre of the ring is clearly at the coordinate origin O(0,0). The circle is drawn as a solid line, defining its complete circular shape. Points b and d are symmetrically placed on the y‑axis; although the radius is not explicitly given, the figure suggests a uniform radius R.

On the right side, the problem text in Chinese describes the kinematic equation of the ring’s projection: $(x‑vt)^2+y^2=R^2$, indicating that the ring moves uniformly in the positive x‑direction with speed v. The problem asks for an analysis of two optical‑reflection propositions and may involve deriving coordinates of certain points.

The overall image is clear and concise, combining geometric figures with algebraic equations to present the scenario of an optical problem involving a moving ring.

This picture shows the solution process of a physics problem, mainly involving special relativity and the geometric‑optical properties of an ellipse. First, an inertial frame S′ that is stationary relative to the ring is introduced. Through coordinate transformation, the static equation of the ring in the S′ frame is obtained as a stationary elliptical ring; the ellipse’s parameters and the coordinates of its two foci are given. Then, two geometric‑optical properties of a stationary ellipse are explained. Next, based on symmetry, the reflection of light rays emitted from the centre of the ring (moving with the ring) in the S frame is analyzed. Finally, via Lorentz transformation, the coordinates of the projections of the foci of the S′ frame in the S frame at time t are derived, and a conclusion is drawn by combining the geometric‑optical properties.
problem_answer:
(1) 否。随环状物一起运动的环心点发出的所有光线，经环内壁反射后并非都过环心点。
(2) 能。焦点坐标为：$F_1\left(\dfrac{v}{c}R+vt,\,0\right)$，$F_2\left(-\dfrac{v}{c}R+vt,\,0\right)$。
